context: Tests whether the agent implements the email-first checkout entry step correctly: collecting only the email initially, validating it properly, checking for an existing account, and offering both login and continue-as-guest options without ever blocking the checkout path.

checklist.name: Email only at entry
checklist.description: The component renders only an email input field (and a submit/continue button) as its initial visible form — no password, name, or other account fields are present in the initial state

checklist.name: Email regex validation
checklist.description: The component validates the email using a regex pattern equivalent to /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/ before calling the check-email API or invoking any callback

checklist.name: API check-email called
checklist.description: The component calls /api/auth/check-email via a POST request with a JSON body containing the email to check whether an account exists

checklist.name: Both options shown for existing account
checklist.description: When the API returns { exists: true }, the component displays both a 'Log in' option and a 'Continue as guest' option — not just one

checklist.name: Guest proceeds without login prompt
checklist.description: When the API returns { exists: false }, the component calls onContinueAsGuest(email) directly, without showing a login prompt

checklist.name: Checkout not blocked
checklist.description: There is no code path that prevents checkout from proceeding — a user with an existing account can choose 'Continue as guest' and the onContinueAsGuest callback is invoked

checklist.name: onLogin callback used
checklist.description: Clicking the 'Log in' option invokes the onLogin(email) prop callback (not a navigation redirect or inline form expansion)

checklist.name: Loading/disabled state
checklist.description: The continue button is disabled or shows a loading indicator while the check-email API call is in-flight (prevents double-submission)

checklist.name: autoComplete email
checklist.description: The email input element has autoComplete="email" set

checklist.name: Enter key triggers continue
checklist.description: Pressing the Enter key in the email input triggers the same continue/check action as clicking the button
